name: BTC 15m Trend Pullback Reaccept
edge_rationale: In a confirmed 4h uptrend, a controlled multi-bar pullback into a pre-existing 15m equal-low support level followed by a 15m close back above that frozen level may indicate trend-continuation reacceptance without requiring liquidity-sweep geometry.
counterparty_or_market_mechanism: Weak longs and late counter-trend shorts transact into the pullback near visible support. A subsequent close back above the frozen level can trap late shorts and confirm that the pullback was absorbed without making CVD, force orders, or OI the primary edge.
frozen_assumptions: No production, PAPER, runtime, core, execution, orchestrator, or settings changes.
15m trigger evaluation uses closed bars only; entry is the next 15m open.
4h EMA trend filter uses completed 4h candles only.
Equal-low support must be frozen at least 5 completed 15m bars before the trigger bar.
Pullback must occur before the trigger bar, not only as a wick on the trigger bar.
CVD, funding, OI, and force-order fields are diagnostic-only and cannot decide pass/fail.
No post-hoc threshold rescue after seeing OOS results.
Exit model is fixed before run: stop below frozen support, fixed-R target, and max-hold timeout.
expected_observation: If the setup works, it should produce at least 60 OOS-equivalent trades, ER >= 1.5, PF >= 1.8, max drawdown <= 6R, acceptable cost sensitivity, and low overlap with trial-00095 while preserving a distinct trend-pullback mechanism.

kill_criteria.methodology_bug: Any lookahead, future structure confirmation, mutable OOS protocol, or data leakage invalidates the run.

failure_modes: Frozen equal-low levels are too broad and capture generic pullbacks instead of meaningful support.
Reacceptance close occurs too late and produces poor R/R at the next 15m open.
TFI confirmation adds no incremental value over structure plus 4h trend.
Trades concentrate in one month or volatility cluster.
Overlap with trial-00095 is high, meaning the setup is not a distinct portfolio candidate.
out_of_scope: OI, funding, or force orders as scoring or trigger.
Adaptive optimization, Optuna search, or LLM-selected thresholds.
author: Codex
